question: What does `git stash` do?
options: Temporarily shelves changes so you can work on something else
Creates a new branch with your current changes
Merges your changes into the main branch
explanation: git stash saves your uncommitted changes on a stack and reverts your working directory to the last commit. Run `git stash pop` to bring them back.

question: What is the difference between `git fetch` and `git pull`?
options: They are exactly the same command
fetch downloads changes but does not merge; pull downloads and merges
pull downloads changes but does not merge; fetch downloads and merges
fetch only works on branches, pull works on tags too
explanation: `git fetch` downloads remote changes into your local repo without touching your working branch. `git pull` does fetch + merge (or rebase) in one step.

question: What does `git rebase -i HEAD~3` let you do?
options: Interactively edit, squash, or reorder the last 3 commits
Create a branch from 3 commits ago
Cherry-pick the last 3 commits onto another branch
explanation: Interactive rebase opens an editor where you can squash, reword, edit, or drop any of the last 3 commits before pushing — great for cleaning up history.
